message:
feat(delete,routing): add cross-repo routing support to delete command

Extend the delete command to handle issue IDs that resolve to external
repositories via the routing configuration. When any supplied ID belongs
to an external workspace the command now fans out into per-route batches
via a new `execute_routed` path, mirroring the pattern already used by
other routed commands.

Key changes in src/cli/commands/delete.rs:
- Detect external routes after discovering the beads dir and delegate to
  `execute_routed` when any batch is external.
- Add `PreparedDeleteRoute` struct to carry per-batch resolution results
  (resolved IDs, blocked dependents, cascade set, final delete list).
- `prepare_delete_route`: resolves IDs with `IdResolver`, collects
  dependents/cascade for one route without mutating storage.
- `apply_delete_route`: performs the actual tombstone/purge mutations,
  removes dependency links, clears last-touched, and triggers auto-flush
  for external repos (non-fatal on failure).
- `execute_routed`: aggregates previews and results across all routes,
  respects --dry-run, --force, --cascade, and all output modes
  (JSON/TOON/quiet/human).
- `render_routed_delete_preview`: human-readable preview for the
  multi-route case, including cascade and orphan breakdowns.
- `merge_delete_result` / `finalize_delete_result`: combine per-batch
  `DeleteResult`s and deduplicate before final render.
- `clear_last_touched_if_deleted` / `sorted_unique_strings`: small
  helpers extracted for clarity.
- Local (non-routed) path gains `IdResolver`-based resolution to align
  with other commands; explicit not-found loop removed (resolver handles
  it).

New tests in tests/e2e_routing.rs:
- `e2e_routing_delete_external_issue_via_main_workspace`: verifies that
  a routed partial ID resolves and tombstones the issue in the external
  workspace when invoked from the main workspace.
- `e2e_routing_delete_preview_does_not_mutate_earlier_local_batch`:
  verifies that a blocked-dependent preview (where the external batch
  cannot proceed) leaves both the local and external issues untouched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
